codeReview.summary: The change improves test isolation by setting HOME and serializing env-mutating tests, but it does not fully isolate token resolution across environments and still leaves an env-restore edge case.

issues.description: The tests only override HOME, but auth path resolution uses dirs::config_dir() and Linux explicitly respects XDG_CONFIG_HOME. If XDG_CONFIG_HOME points to a real config dir containing spikes/auth.toml, DataSource::new(true) can still resolve a token, so test_data_source_remote_without_token remains machine-dependent.
HOME is restored only when original_home is Some(...). If HOME was initially unset, the test leaves HOME set to a temporary directory that gets deleted, leaking process env state to later tests.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Shared guidance documents isolated HOME usage for manual validation but does not document cross-platform unit-test isolation when config lookup honors XDG_CONFIG_HOME; this gap likely contributed to HOME-only isolation in the fix.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/user-testing.md:86 recommends isolated HOME; cli/src/auth.rs:164 states Linux auth path respects XDG_CONFIG_HOME; cli/src/commands/mcp.rs:2195, 2229, 2256 only override HOME.

summary: Review failed: the feature partially addresses auth.toml interference, but test isolation is still incomplete for XDG_CONFIG_HOME environments and has an env-restore edge case.
